update_every.title: Update every
update_every.description: Data collection interval, measured in seconds.

autodetection_retry.title: Detection retry
autodetection_retry.description: Recheck interval in seconds. Zero means no recheck will be scheduled.

url.title: URL
url.description: The URL of the Prometheus metrics endpoint.

timeout.title: Timeout
timeout.description: The timeout in seconds for the HTTP request.

not_follow_redirects.title: Not follow redirects
not_follow_redirects.description: If set, the client will not follow HTTP redirects automatically.

expected_prefix.title: Expected prefix
expected_prefix.description: If set, the job's check passes only when at least one scraped metric name starts with this prefix. Guards against scraping an unexpected endpoint.

app.title: Application
app.description: Application name used as the app segment of chart contexts ('prometheus.{app}.{metric}'). When unset, it is taken from a matched profile, otherwise it falls back to the job name.

vnode.title: Vnode
vnode.description: Associates this data collection job with a [Virtual Node](https://learn.netdata.cloud/docs/netdata-agent/configuration/organize-systems-metrics-and-alerts#virtual-nodes).

selector.title: Selectors
selector.description: Configuration for selecting and filtering a set of time series using Prometheus selectors. If left empty, no filtering is applied.

allow.title: Allow
allow.description: Allow time series that match any of the specified [selectors](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/tree/master/src/go/pkg/prometheus/selector#readme).

deny.title: Deny
deny.description: Deny time series that match any of the specified [selectors](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/tree/master/src/go/pkg/prometheus/selector#readme).

relabeling.title: Metric relabeling
relabeling.description: Prometheus-compatible metric relabeling applied before charts are built. Each block scopes its rules to the metrics whose name matches `match`. See the [relabeling reference](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/tree/master/src/go/plugin/go.d/collector/prometheus/relabel#readme) for the full syntax and examples.

match.title: Match
match.description: Netdata simple patterns matched against the full metric name (including any _bucket/_sum/_count suffix, so prefer globs like `app_lat*`). Use `*` to target every metric.

metric_relabel_configs.title: Rules
metric_relabel_configs.description: Prometheus relabel rules applied, in order, to the matched metrics.

source_labels.title: Source labels
source_labels.description: Labels whose values are joined (by separator) to form the rule input.

separator.title: Separator
separator.description: Separator used to join source label values.

regex.title: Regex
regex.description: Regular expression matched against the joined source label values.

target_label.title: Target label
target_label.description: Label (or `__name__` for the metric name) written by the action.

replacement.title: Replacement
replacement.description: Replacement value (regex capture groups allowed, e.g. `${1}`).

profiles.title: Profiles
profiles.description: Curated, exporter-specific chart profiles. `auto` (default) selects every profile whose match hits a scraped metric; `exact` selects only the named profiles (each must match); `combined` is auto plus the named profiles; `none` disables profiles (generic autogen charts only). A selected profile may use profile-root `autogen.exclude` to suppress fallback charts for unmatched source families while retaining their samples.

entries.title: Profile entries
entries.description: Profiles to select. Each must match at least one scraped metric or the job fails its check.

name.title: Profile basename
name.description: Profile file basename: lowercase letters, digits, or underscores, starting with a letter.

entries.title: Profile entries
entries.description: Profiles to select in addition to the auto-selected ones.

name.title: Profile basename
name.description: Profile file basename: lowercase letters, digits, or underscores, starting with a letter.

max_time_series.title: Time series limit
max_time_series.description: If an endpoint returns more time series than this limit, the data is not processed. Set to 0 for no limit.

max_time_series_per_metric.title: Time series per metric limit
max_time_series_per_metric.description: Metrics with more time series than this limit are skipped. Set to 0 for no limit.

fallback_type.title: Untyped metrics fallback
fallback_type.description: Process Untyped metrics as Counter or Gauge instead of ignoring them.

gauge.title: As Gauge
gauge.description: Untyped metrics matching any [pattern](https://golang.org/pkg/path/filepath/#Match) will be processed as Gauge.

counter.title: As Counter
counter.description: Untyped metrics matching any [pattern](https://golang.org/pkg/path/filepath/#Match) will be processed as Counter.

bearer_token_file.title: Bearer token file
bearer_token_file.description: The path to the file with Bearer token.

proxy_url.title: Proxy URL
proxy_url.description: The URL of the proxy server.

headers.title: Headers
headers.description: Additional HTTP headers to include in the request.

tls_skip_verify.title: Skip TLS verification
tls_skip_verify.description: If set, TLS certificate verification will be skipped.

tls_ca.title: TLS CA
tls_ca.description: The path to the CA certificate file for TLS verification.

tls_cert.title: TLS certificate
tls_cert.description: The path to the client certificate file for TLS authentication.

tls_key.title: TLS key
tls_key.description: The path to the client key file for TLS authentication.

force_http2.title: Force HTTP2
force_http2.description: If set, forces the use of HTTP/2 protocol for all requests, even over plain TCP (h2c).

autodetection_retry.ui:help: This option determines how frequently (in seconds) Netdata will retry data collection jobs that failed initially, with the value of 60 meaning it retries to start data collection jobs every 60 seconds, while setting it to 0 disables this retry mechanism entirely.

vnode.ui:placeholder: To use this option, first create a Virtual Node and then reference its name here.

timeout.ui:help: Accepts decimals for precise control (e.g., type 1.5 for 1.5 seconds).

selector.ui:help: The logic is as follows: `(allow1 OR allow2) AND !(deny1 OR deny2)`.
